front.title: Orbital Relics
card_text: Glittering stardust clouds orbit Earth, beautiful relics of our greatest space adventures.
Jagged metal shards zip through the void, threatening to crush our newest satellites.

scoring_anchors.3_pts: Identified relic vs. shard contrast.
scoring_anchors.7_pts: Detected 'adventure' vs. 'threat' framing.
scoring_anchors.10_pts: Check for 'Space Junk' vs 'Space Heritage' terminology.

back.reality_anchor: NASA orbital debris vs space archaeology

teacher_notes.deep_insight: Science reporting often chooses between an 'Explorer's Lens' (focusing on history) and a 'Safety Lens' (focusing on risk). Both describe the same satellite fragments, but one makes you feel nostalgic while the other makes you feel worried.
teacher_notes.habit: When you see something called a 'relic,' ask if it’s actually 'trash.'

front.title: Ant City Secrets
card_text: A brilliant empire of tiny architects builds a complex city beneath your feet.
A relentless army of millions marches across the yard, destroying every leaf in sight.

scoring_anchors.3_pts: Note city-building vs. army marching.
scoring_anchors.7_pts: Analyze 'brilliant' vs. 'relentless' descriptors.
scoring_anchors.10_pts: Scan for 'civilization' vs 'pest' framing.

back.reality_anchor: Ant colony intelligence vs invasive species reports

teacher_notes.deep_insight: Nature writers use 'Civic' language to build respect for insects and 'Military' language to justify getting rid of them. The behavior—moving leaves and building tunnels—is the same in both accounts.
teacher_notes.habit: Identify the 'Role.' Is the insect being cast as a worker or a soldier?

front.title: Earth’s Chimney
card_text: Giant vents recycle the planet's crust, cooling the Earth and creating fertile islands.
Molten rock explodes from the peak, burying entire forests under a river of fire.

scoring_anchors.3_pts: Note 'vent' vs. 'explosion' contrast.
scoring_anchors.7_pts: Detect planetary health vs. local disaster focus.
scoring_anchors.10_pts: Compare long-term geological vs short-term human impact.

back.reality_anchor: Volcanic soil fertility vs eruption damage

teacher_notes.deep_insight: This is a lens of 'Time.' A geologist looks at millions of years and sees life-giving 'recycling.' A news reporter looks at 24 hours and sees 'fire and death.' Both are accurate for their specific timeframe.
teacher_notes.habit: Ask: 'Is this story about the next ten minutes or the next ten thousand years?'

references.title: BBC: The Deadly Power of Volcanoes

front.title: Clean Wind
card_text: Elegant white sails catch the breeze to light up homes without any smoke.
Steel towers spin heavy blades, casting shadows and blocking the mountain view.

scoring_anchors.3_pts: Note 'sails' vs. 'steel towers.'
scoring_anchors.7_pts: Analyze 'clean' vs. 'view-blocking' framing.
scoring_anchors.10_pts: Identify benefit for 'city' vs. cost for 'local'.

back.reality_anchor: Wind turbine efficiency vs visual impact

teacher_notes.deep_insight: The 'Green' narrative focuses on the invisible benefit (carbon reduction), while the 'Local' narrative focuses on the visible change (the view). Outlets often pick one, making the project seem like a pure gift or a pure theft.
teacher_notes.habit: If a report only mentions 'clean air,' search for what the neighbors of the project think.

front.title: The Library Portal
card_text: A glowing portal gives every child the keys to the world’s knowledge.
A buzzing hive of loud voices fights for every second of your attention.

scoring_anchors.3_pts: Note 'knowledge' vs. 'attention' contrast.
scoring_anchors.7_pts: Detect 'portal' vs. 'hive' imagery.
scoring_anchors.10_pts: Check if user is treated as a student or a product.

back.reality_anchor: Internet educational benefits vs attention economy

teacher_notes.deep_insight: Educational media frames the internet as a 'Library' (you choose the info). Media literacy reports frame it as a 'Casino' (the info chooses you). Both describe the same screen and the same internet connection.
teacher_notes.habit: Ask: 'Am I searching for something, or is the app trying to find me?'

front.title: Battery Floor
card_text: Minerals from the seafloor provide batteries for millions of clean cars.
Industrial machines crush undiscovered species in the last untouched wilderness.

scoring_anchors.3_pts: Note batteries vs. wilderness contrast.
scoring_anchors.7_pts: Identify 'climate solution' vs. 'habitat destruction' framing.
scoring_anchors.10_pts: Trace which 'Green' value is being prioritized.

back.reality_anchor: Deep sea mining ISA regulations debate

teacher_notes.deep_insight: This is a conflict between two environmental goals: 'Stopping Climate Change' vs. 'Preserving Biodiversity.' Outlets will often frame the choice so that only one goal seems to exist, making the decision seem much easier than it actually is.
teacher_notes.habit: When a solution is 'Green,' ask: 'What color is the habitat it's replacing?'

front.title: Robo-Chauffeur
card_text: Tireless algorithms promise a future with zero traffic accidents.
Software makes life-or-death road choices without any human accountability.

scoring_anchors.3_pts: Note safety vs. accountability contrast.
scoring_anchors.7_pts: Detect 'tireless' (hero) vs. 'black-box' (risk) framing.
scoring_anchors.10_pts: Cross-reference technical safety data with legal liability.

back.reality_anchor: Autonomous vehicle safety vs moral machine ethics

teacher_notes.deep_insight: Tech outlets report on 'Statistical Safety' (fewer total deaths), while legal/human rights outlets report on 'Individual Agency' (who is to blame for one death?). A car that is 99% safer can still be 100% terrifying if there is no human in control.
teacher_notes.habit: If a machine is 'smarter,' ask: 'Who gets in trouble when the machine makes a mistake?'

references.title: The Verge: The Ethics of AI Driving

front.title: Cultured Protein
card_text: Sterile lab-grown meat saves thousands of animals from the slaughterhouse.
Vats of chemical broth produce synthetic products to replace farm-raised food.

scoring_anchors.3_pts: Note saving animals vs. synthetic broth.
scoring_anchors.7_pts: Analyze 'sterile' (clean) vs. 'chemical' (artificial) framing.
scoring_anchors.10_pts: Check for industry-sponsored terminology.

back.reality_anchor: Lab-grown meat FDA approval vs naming controversy

teacher_notes.deep_insight: Nomenclature is a battlefield. 'Cultured' or 'Cell-based' meat sounds like a breakthrough; 'Synthetic' or 'Lab-grown' sounds like a warning. The same science—multiplying animal cells in a liquid—is framed as either 'Kindness' or 'Chemistry.'
teacher_notes.habit: Identify the 'Yuck Factor.' Is the author using words that make the food sound like a lab experiment or a home-cooked meal?

references.title: The Guardian: The Future of Food

front.title: Digital Sovereign
card_text: Instant digital payments bring banking to billions without traditional accounts.
Government-tracked code replaces cash, allowing the state to monitor every cent.

scoring_anchors.3_pts: Contrast inclusion vs. monitoring.
scoring_anchors.7_pts: Detect 'empowerment' vs. 'control' framing.
scoring_anchors.10_pts: Proposed habit: Trace anonymity levels in digital currencies.

back.reality_anchor: CBDC financial inclusion vs privacy risks

teacher_notes.deep_insight: Central Bank Digital Currencies (CBDCs) are framed by global banks as 'Tools of Inclusion' (helping the poor). Privacy advocates frame them as 'Tools of Totalitarianism' (ending financial privacy). Both perspectives are factually grounded in how the software code is actually written.
teacher_notes.habit: Ask: 'If this tool makes my life easier, does it also make it easier for someone to stop me from using it?'

references.title: Reuters: Privacy concerns over digital Euro

front.title: The Fast Forest
card_text: Millions of saplings are planted to soak up carbon and cool Earth.
Rows of non-native trees create a silent desert where wildlife cannot survive.

scoring_anchors.3_pts: Note 'cooling Earth' vs. 'silent desert.'
scoring_anchors.7_pts: Analyze carbon-counting vs. ecosystem-health framing.
scoring_anchors.10_pts: Cross-reference tree-planting numbers with species diversity.

back.reality_anchor: Monoculture reforestation vs biodiversity loss

teacher_notes.deep_insight: This is a conflict of 'Metrics.' A climate report measures 'Tons of Carbon' (a win). A biology report measures 'Species Diversity' (a loss). Both reports are looking at the same 10,000 trees but are using different rulers to measure success.
teacher_notes.habit: When you hear about 'Millions of Trees,' ask: 'Are they all the same kind, and what lived there before they arrived?'

references.title: BBC: The problem with tree planting
The Economist: Reforestation and its Discontents

front.title: Wolf Return
card_text: Silver hunters rebalance the valley, and willow, beaver, and songbird life surges back.
Shadowy predators cross fence lines at dusk, leaving ranchers guarding calves through the night.

scoring_anchors.3_pts: Spotted ecosystem healer vs. livestock danger.
scoring_anchors.7_pts: Noted valley-wide gains vs. household losses.
scoring_anchors.10_pts: Ask who benefits—and who pays.

back.reality_anchor: National Geographic Yellowstone wolves Reuters ranchers

teacher_notes.deep_insight: The same wolf can be framed through an 'Ecosystem Lens' or a 'Livelihood Lens.' Conservation reporting zooms out to rivers, trees, and biodiversity. Rural reporting zooms in to one family, one herd, and one night of risk. Each lens serves a different audience and value system.
teacher_notes.habit: Zoom out, then zoom in: what changes for the forest, and what changes for one family?

references.title: National Geographic: How wolves change rivers and ecosystems
Reuters: Ranchers face wolf return in Colorado

front.title: Thirst Machine
card_text: Sea water turns sweet, rescuing crowded cities as reservoirs shrink under relentless heat.
Pipes dump dense brine back to sea, and the miracle sip comes with a heavy power bill.

scoring_anchors.3_pts: Saw lifesaver water vs. waste plume.
scoring_anchors.7_pts: Tracked drought relief vs. energy/ecology framing.
scoring_anchors.10_pts: Check what problem gets moved, not solved.

back.reality_anchor: Reuters desalination drought Scientific American brine

teacher_notes.deep_insight: Desalination stories often use a 'Scarcity Lens' or an 'Externalities Lens.' One celebrates water security for cities. The other asks where the salt, energy demand, and marine stress go. The frame serves either urgent human need or long-term system accounting.
teacher_notes.habit: When a miracle resource appears, ask what it consumes and where the leftovers go.

references.title: Scientific American: The environmental downside of desalination

front.title: Border Carbon Wall
card_text: Carbon tolls shield cleaner factories, stopping dirty imports from undercutting local climate rules.
Green tariffs rise at the border, squeezing poorer exporters that did little to cause the crisis.

scoring_anchors.3_pts: Spotted fair-competition vs. unfair-burden lens.
scoring_anchors.7_pts: Noted domestic industry protection inside climate language.
scoring_anchors.10_pts: Ask who gains leverage when standards travel.

back.reality_anchor: EU carbon border tax Reuters Economist

teacher_notes.deep_insight: Carbon border taxes sit at the crossroads of climate policy, trade policy, and geopolitics. One lens treats them as fairness tools: local firms with stricter rules should not be undercut by dirtier imports. Another treats them as green protectionism that shifts costs onto poorer exporters. The chosen frame often serves either domestic industry or climate-justice concerns.
teacher_notes.habit: When policy sounds moral, inspect the money path and ask who gets bargaining power.

references.title: Reuters: EU launches carbon border tariff system
The Economist: The trouble with carbon border taxes

front.title: ULTRA-GUM WILL EXPLODE!
card_text: A scary rumor is spreading! Kids who chew Ultra-Gum might have it POP in their mouths like a giant water balloon. Everyone is throwing their gum away before it's too late!

scoring_anchors.3_pts: The news is spreading fear and surprise.
scoring_anchors.7_pts: The outcome could be kids wasting their favorite candy.
scoring_anchors.10_pts: The intention behind the rumor is to cause harmless chaos.

back.reality_anchor: How silly rumors start

teacher_notes.deep_insight: Even silly stories can feel very real in the moment, making us act without thinking.
teacher_notes.habit: When you feel scared by a rumor, ask 'Is that even physically possible?'

front.title: SPARKLE-KITTEN NEEDS YOU!
card_text: The world's cutest kitten is crying and all alone! It’s cold and sad. Share this picture right now to send it good vibes and love, or else you’re totally heartless!

scoring_anchors.3_pts: The news is spreading sadness and guilt.
scoring_anchors.7_pts: The outcome could be thousands of useless post shares.
scoring_anchors.10_pts: The intention behind the post is to farm likes and attention.

back.reality_anchor: How emotional posts get shares

teacher_notes.deep_insight: Feeling sad for an animal makes us want to help, and sharing feels like helping, even if it doesn't do anything real.
teacher_notes.habit: Stop and ask, 'How does sharing this actually help?'

references.title: The Power of Cute Animals Online

front.title: ROBOT INVASION TODAY!
card_text: A giant toy robot is stomping through the city! People are screaming! Watch this exclusive video right now before it destroys everything!

scoring_anchors.3_pts: The news is spreading shock and excitement.
scoring_anchors.7_pts: The outcome could be people clicking dangerous links.
scoring_anchors.10_pts: The intention behind the story is to get advertising money.

back.reality_anchor: How clickbait works

teacher_notes.deep_insight: Our brain pays extra attention to things that look dangerous, which is why clickbait works.
teacher_notes.habit: If it sounds like a movie, it might be a trick to get a click.

front.title: ICE CREAM IS FORBIDDEN!
card_text: Your principal just made a new rule: ICE CREAM IS BANNED FOREVER at school! This is so unfair! We have to protest during lunch right now!

scoring_anchors.3_pts: The news is spreading anger and unfairness.
scoring_anchors.7_pts: The outcome could be students getting into trouble.
scoring_anchors.10_pts: The intention behind the message is to start a protest.

back.reality_anchor: Making people mad to get attention

teacher_notes.deep_insight: Anger makes us act fast. If someone says something is unfair, check the official facts first.
teacher_notes.habit: Don't get mad until you hear it from the actual source.

front.title: FREE GALAXY-PHONE TICKET!
card_text: You’re one of the lucky 10! Click here now to claim your golden ticket and win a brand new Galaxy-Phone instantly! This is your only chance!

scoring_anchors.3_pts: The news is spreading greed and urgency.
scoring_anchors.7_pts: The outcome could be giving away personal information.
scoring_anchors.10_pts: The intention behind the scam is to steal data.

back.reality_anchor: How phishing scams trick you

teacher_notes.deep_insight: The feeling of being 'lucky' can make us forget to be careful and safe online.
teacher_notes.habit: If it sounds too good to be true, it probably is.

front.title: LOCAL WATER SUPPLY TAINTED
card_text: URGENT: Traces of toxic chemical X found in city reservoir! Official reports are being suppressed. Do NOT drink tap water until further notice. Share to save lives!

scoring_anchors.3_pts: The news is spreading immediate terror.
scoring_anchors.7_pts: The outcome could be dangerous stockpiling and shortages.
scoring_anchors.10_pts: The intention behind the alert is to create public distrust.

back.reality_anchor: Amplifying real-world anxieties

teacher_notes.deep_insight: This leverages our fundamental need for safety and uses 'suppressed reports' to build distrust.
teacher_notes.habit: Wait for official emergency alerts before reacting.

front.title: MASSIVE LAYOFFS ANNOUNCED
card_text: Top company XYZ just fired 10,000 workers via email! Is your job next? Analysts warn a massive economic crash is imminent. Your financial future is at risk.

scoring_anchors.3_pts: The news is spreading deep financial worry.
scoring_anchors.7_pts: The outcome could be a stock market crash.
scoring_anchors.10_pts: The intention behind the warning is to manipulate markets.

back.reality_anchor: Using fear to influence financial markets

teacher_notes.deep_insight: News about money and job security instantly activates our survival instincts.
teacher_notes.habit: Diversify your sources. One layoff doesn't equal a collapse.

references.title: The Impact of Fear on Economics

front.title: NEW VIRUS IS AIRBORNE
card_text: A shocking new report suggests the 'K-Flu' virus spreads purely through the air, even outdoors! Vaccines might be useless. Stay locked indoors and prepare!

scoring_anchors.3_pts: The news is spreading health-related dread.
scoring_anchors.7_pts: The outcome could be social withdrawal and isolation.
scoring_anchors.10_pts: The intention behind the report is to undermine authorities.

back.reality_anchor: Leveraging uncertainty during health crises

teacher_notes.deep_insight: Health fears are universal. This leverages uncertainty to create paralysis and isolation.
teacher_notes.habit: Check information with established health organizations like the WHO.

front.title: ELECTION WAS RIGGED
card_text: Leaked emails prove 'glitches' in voting machines flipped thousands of votes! Your democracy is under siege. They are stealing your future! Rise up and fight!

scoring_anchors.3_pts: The news is spreading fury and resentment.
scoring_anchors.7_pts: The outcome could be civil unrest and violence.
scoring_anchors.10_pts: The intention behind the leak is to destabilize the nation.

back.reality_anchor: Misinformation to undermine election integrity

teacher_notes.deep_insight: This is a direct attack on civic identity, framing issues as 'Us vs. Them' for control.
teacher_notes.habit: Demand evidence and independent audits before believing fraud claims.

front.title: HIDDEN ALLERGY IN FOOD?
card_text: Is common ingredient 'X' actually a secret neurotoxin? Unconfirmed studies link it to brain damage in children. Are companies poisoning your family for profit?

scoring_anchors.3_pts: The news is spreading parental guilt and panic.
scoring_anchors.7_pts: The outcome could be harmful diet changes for kids.
scoring_anchors.10_pts: The intention behind the study is to hijack engagement via fear.

back.reality_anchor: Leveraging parental anxiety about health

teacher_notes.deep_insight: This targets the responsibility of protecting family to weaponize scientific uncertainty.
teacher_notes.habit: Check if studies are peer-reviewed before changing habits.

references.title: How to Read a Scientific Study

front.title: SCHOOL JUICE RECALL
card_text: Posts warn fruit pouches in classrooms could make kids faint by lunch. Families rush stores, and backpacks fill with emergency drinks.

scoring_anchors.3_pts: The news is spreading fear.
scoring_anchors.7_pts: The outcome could be panic buying.
scoring_anchors.10_pts: The intention behind the post is to trigger sharing.

back.reality_anchor: school juice scare rumor

teacher_notes.deep_insight: Kid-centered safety scares spread fast because adults want to protect children immediately, and kids copy the panic they see around them.
teacher_notes.habit: Pause before you pass it on: check the school or district message first.

references.title: AAP: Talking to Children About Scary News
Common Sense Media: Helping Kids Spot Misinformation

front.title: ATM LINES GROW
card_text: Viral clips claim regional banks will freeze withdrawals this weekend. Crowds drain cash machines before markets even open.

scoring_anchors.3_pts: The news is spreading economic fear.
scoring_anchors.7_pts: The outcome could be cash hoarding.
scoring_anchors.10_pts: The intention behind the clips is to spark a bank run.

back.reality_anchor: bank run rumor withdrawals false

teacher_notes.deep_insight: Financial rumors exploit scarcity thinking and social proof: seeing other people rush makes danger feel confirmed before facts arrive.
teacher_notes.habit: When money panic trends, verify with regulators and major outlets before moving funds.

references.title: Reuters: How social media can accelerate bank runs

front.title: COUNT DELAY LEAK
card_text: Audio 'proves' officials plan to pause vote counting in key cities. Feeds fill with calls to surround election offices tonight.

scoring_anchors.3_pts: The news is spreading rage.
scoring_anchors.7_pts: The outcome could be intimidation and unrest.
scoring_anchors.10_pts: The intention behind the audio is to break trust in results.

back.reality_anchor: election count delay rumor audio

teacher_notes.deep_insight: Civic rumors target legitimacy itself. Once people feel the process is stolen, they may accept escalation before evidence is checked.
teacher_notes.habit: Treat leaked election media as unverified until confirmed by officials, audits, and multiple trusted outlets.
